disclaimer: Illustrative regulatory-awareness items compiled from publicly announced regulatory direction. Not a record of specific enforcement actions against any named entity. Verify every item against the original SEBI / BSE / NGT / CPCB / MoEFCC / BEE notification before acting. Not legal advice.

events.title: BRSR Core assurance scope expanding to a wider set of listed companies
events.summary: SEBI's phased BRSR Core framework extends mandatory third-party (reasonable/limited) assurance to progressively more of the top listed companies by market capitalisation. Affected companies should plan to engage a SEBI-recognised assurance provider ahead of their applicable filing year. Confirm exact applicability and timelines against the latest SEBI circular.

events.title: Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS) — compliance mechanism rollout
events.summary: India's CCTS compliance mechanism is being operationalised in phases on the BEE platform for energy-intensive sectors such as cement, steel, aluminium, petrochemicals and thermal power. Obligated entities should begin preparing baseline GHG inventories. Confirm sector inclusion and submission deadlines against the official MoEFCC/BEE notification.

events.title: Continued NGT scrutiny of effluent compliance in the Indo-Gangetic belt
events.summary: The National Green Tribunal continues to scrutinise industrial effluent compliance with CPCB norms across northern river-basin states, with water-intensive sectors (textiles, tannery, food processing) most exposed. Units should ensure effluent treatment and CPCB consent conditions are current. Verify any specific order against the NGT record.

events.title: BRSR disclosure template continues to evolve (Scope 3, water, pay-gap fields)
events.summary: The BRSR reporting template is periodically revised to add fields such as selected Scope 3 categories, water stewardship and gender pay-gap disclosures. Companies should track template updates each filing cycle and confirm the current format against the BSE/SEBI notice before filing.

events.title: EPR enforcement on plastic-packaging obligations is tightening
events.summary: CPCB continues to step up enforcement of Extended Producer Responsibility obligations under the Plastic Waste Management Rules, including for packaging-intensive sectors such as cement. Producers, importers and brand-owners should ensure EPR registration and target fulfilment are up to date. This is a general compliance awareness note, not a record of action against any specific company.

events.title: ESG-rating disclosure expectations for ESG-themed funds
events.summary: SEBI's framework for ESG schemes points toward greater disclosure of constituent ESG ratings sourced from SEBI-registered ESG Rating Providers. Index-fund/ETF and ESG-scheme managers should track the applicable disclosure requirements. Confirm scope and effective dates against the official SEBI circular.

events.title: Siting and air-quality conditions tightening near critically polluted areas
events.summary: Environmental clearance and air-quality conditions for new coal-based capacity near CPCB-designated Critically Polluted Areas remain under close regulatory and tribunal scrutiny. Operators in or near such areas should keep air-quality improvement plans current. Verify any specific direction against the NGT/MoEFCC record.

events.title: BEE PAT energy-efficiency cycles — designated-consumer obligations continue
events.summary: BEE's Perform, Achieve and Trade (PAT) scheme sets energy-efficiency targets across designated energy-intensive sectors (steel, cement, aluminium, chlor-alkali, fertilizer, paper, glass and others). Designated Consumers should track registration and target requirements for the applicable cycle on the BEE portal.

events.title: Stewardship and engagement disclosure expectations for ESG funds
events.summary: ESG-themed funds are expected to maintain and publish stewardship/engagement approaches covering climate, board diversity and executive pay. AMCs should ensure their published policies are current. Confirm exact requirements against the applicable SEBI circular.

events.title: Grid emission factor updates affect Scope 2 calculations
events.summary: India's grid emission factor is updated periodically (CEA CO2 Baseline Database / national inventory). Companies using location-based Scope 2 accounting should refresh their factor each year so BRSR and GHG figures stay current. Use the latest published CEA/MoEFCC factor for your reporting year.
